info.title: Actions Events
info.description: OpenAPI specification for Auth0 Actions trigger events

accessToken.description: Information about the access token to be issued.

client.description: Information about the Client used during this token exchange.

client_id.description: The client id of the application the user is logging in to.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

name.description: The name of the application (as defined in the Dashboard).

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

organization.description: Details about the Organization associated with the current transaction.

display_name.description: The friendly name of the Organization.

name.description: The name of the Organization.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

body.description: The body of the POST request. This data will only be available during refresh token, Client Credential Exchange flows and PreUserRegistration Action.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

resource_server.description: Information about the Resource Server that is issuing the access token.

identifier.description: The identifier of the resource server. For example: `https://your-api.example.com`.

tenant.description: Information about the Tenant used during this token exchange.

transaction.description: Information about the Credentials Exchange transaction.

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID can be provided in the initial authentication request when the application redirects to Universal Login. You can use value to correlate logs and requests from your Action code with the user flow.

requested_scopes.description: The scopes specified (if any) when requesting the access token.

target_scopes.description: [Early Access] The live target scope set for the access token. Initialized from the client grants and immediately updated by api.transaction target scope methods across current and subsequent Actions. After all Actions complete, these scopes are intersected with the client grant. Scopes not present in the grant are silently dropped from the final access token.

client.description: Information about the Client with which this login transaction was initiated.

client_id.description: The client id of the application the user is logging in to.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

name.description: The name of the application (as defined in the Dashboard).

connection.description: Details about the Connection that was used to authenticate the user.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

from.description: Email address of the sender for the email.

locale.description: The locale we rendered the message in, example `en_US`, as defined in the BCP-47 specification.

message_type.description: The type of message that is being send, like `verify_email` or `welcome_email`.

subject.description: Subject to be attached to the email.

organization.description: Details about the Organization associated with the current transaction.

display_name.description: The friendly name of the Organization.

name.description: The name of the Organization.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

query.description: The query string parameters sent to the authorization request.

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

friendly_name.description: The friendly name for the tenant, usually a more human-readable version of the ID.

home_url.description: The home URL for the tenant, if defined and as found in its settings.

logo_url.description: The logo URL for the tenant, if defined and as found in its settings.

support_email.description: The email to the tenant's support service, if defined and as found in its settings.

support_url.description: The url to the tenant's support service, if defined and as found in its settings.

transaction.description: Details about the current transaction for tracing purposes.

correlation_id.description: A unique identifier to correlate this request across multiple services for distributed tracing.

user.description: An object describing the user on whose behalf the current transaction was initiated.

app_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that influences the user's access, such as support plan, security roles, or access control groups.

email_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their email address.

name.description: User's full name.

user_id.description: (unique) User's unique identifier.

user_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that does not impact what they can or cannot access, such as work address, home address, or user preferences.

client.description: Information about the Client with which this login transaction was initiated.

client_id.description: The client id of the application the user is logging in to.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

name.description: The name of the application (as defined in the Dashboard).

connection.description: Details about the Connection that was used to authenticate the user.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

as_text.description: The text, as we rendered it, ready to be delivered as a text message.

as_voice.description: The text, as we rendered it, ready to be delivered as a voicetext message.

code.description: The One Time Password that we drawn for this message for some types (e.g. `otp_verify`, `otp_enroll`). If provided, it is important to have it conveyed to the end-user.

delivery_method.description: The way the message should be delivered. Could be `text` or `voice`.

from.description: The E.164 compliant phone number for the sender.

locale.description: The locale we rendered the message in, example `en_US`, as defined in the BCP-47 specification.

message_type.description: The type of message that is being send, like `otp_verify` or `blocked_account`.

recipient.description: The E.164 compliant phone number for the recipient.

organization.description: Details about the Organization associated with the current transaction.

display_name.description: The friendly name of the Organization.

name.description: The name of the Organization.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

friendly_name.description: The friendly name for the tenant, usually a more human-readable version of the ID.

home_url.description: The home URL for the tenant, if defined and as found in its settings.

logo_url.description: The logo URL for the tenant, if defined and as found in its settings.

support_email.description: The email to the tenant's support service, if defined and as found in its settings.

support_url.description: The url to the tenant's support service, if defined and as found in its settings.

transaction.description: Details about the current transaction for tracing purposes.

correlation_id.description: A unique identifier to correlate this request across multiple services for distributed tracing.

user.description: An object describing the user on whose behalf the current transaction was initiated.

app_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that influences the user's access, such as support plan, security roles, or access control groups.

created_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was first created.

email_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their email address.

last_password_reset.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's password was reset/changed. At user creation, this field does not exist. This property is only available for Database connections.

name.description: User's full name.

phone_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their phone number.

updated_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's profile was last updated/modified.

user_id.description: (unique) User's unique identifier.

user_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that does not impact what they can or cannot access, such as work address, home address, or user preferences.

identities.description: Contains info retrieved from the identity provider with which the user originally authenticates. Users may also link their profile to multiple identity providers; those identities will then also appear in this array. The contents of an individual identity provider object varies by provider.

connection.description: Name of the Auth0 connection used to authenticate the user.

isSocial.description: Indicates whether the connection is a social one.

profileData.description: User information associated with the connection. When profiles are linked, it is populated with the associated user info for secondary accounts.

provider.description: Name of the entity that is authenticating the user, such as Facebook, Google, SAML, or your own provider.

user_id.description: User's unique identifier for this connection/provider.

transaction.description: Details about the current custom token exchange transaction.

actor_token.description: The actor token provided in the token exchange request.

actor_token_type.description: The type of the actor token provided in the token exchange request.

actor_token_user.description: The user represented by the actor token. This will only be present if the actor_token_type is urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:id_token and the actor token provided in the token exchange request is a valid Auth0 generated ID token.

app_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that influences the user's access, such as support plan, security roles, or access control groups.

email_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their email address.

last_password_reset.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's password was reset/changed. At user creation, this field does not exist. This property is only available for Database connections.

name.description: User's full name.

phone_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their phone number.

updated_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's profile was last updated/modified.

user_id.description: (unique) User's unique identifier.

user_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that does not impact what they can or cannot access, such as work address, home address, or user preferences.

enrolledFactors.description: An array of authentication factors that the user has enrolled.

items.description: An object describing an enrolled authentication factor type and any factor-specific options.

type.description: The type of authentication factor such as `push-notification`, `phone`, `email`, `otp`, `webauthn-roaming` and `webauthn-platform`.

options.description: Additional options describing this instance of the enrolled factor.

multifactor.description: List of multi-factor authentication (MFA) providers with which the user is enrolled. This array is updated when the user enrolls in MFA and when an administrator resets a user's MFA enrollments.

identities.description: Contains info retrieved from the identity provider with which the user originally authenticates. Users may also link their profile to multiple identity providers; those identities will then also appear in this array. The contents of an individual identity provider object varies by provider.

connection.description: Name of the Auth0 connection used to authenticate the user.

isSocial.description: Indicates whether the connection is a social one.

profileData.description: User information associated with the connection. When profiles are linked, it is populated with the associated user info for secondary accounts.

provider.description: Name of the entity that is authenticating the user, such as Facebook, Google, SAML, or your own provider.

user_id.description: User's unique identifier for this connection/provider.

requested_scopes.description: The scopes requested (if any) provided in the token exchange request.

requested_token_type.description: The type of token to be generated by Auth0. For example: urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token.

subject_token.description: The subject token provided in the token exchange request.

subject_token_type.description: The subject_token_type provided in the token exchange request.

message.description: The CloudEvent message containing all event properties.

type.description: Describes the type of event related to the originating occurrence.

source.description: Identifies the context in which an event happened.

specversion.description: The version of the CloudEvents specification which the event uses.

time.description: Timestamp of when the occurrence happened. Must adhere to RFC 3339.

type.description: This extensibility point is called for both types of requests. Use this field to either create or revoke.

authentication.description: Details about the authentication context for the login.

client.description: Information about the Client with which this login transaction was initiated.

client_id.description: The client id of the application the user is logging in to.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

name.description: The name of the application (as defined in the Dashboard).

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

initial_connection.description: Details about the Connection that was specified to authenticate the user. This represents the initial value as resolved by Auth0 prior to executing this trigger. Calling setConnectionByName will not change this value because it is immutable.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

initial_organization.description: Details about the Organization associated with the current transaction. This represents the initial value as resolved by Auth0 prior to executing this trigger. Calling setOrganization will not change this value because it is immutable.

display_name.description: The friendly name of the Organization.

name.description: The name of the Organization.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

asn.description: The ASN (autonomous system number) of the user-agent making the request.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

query.description: The query string parameters sent to the authorization request.

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

resource_server.description: Details about the resource server to which the access is being requested.

identifier.description: The identifier of the resource server. For example: `https://your-api.example.com`.

security_context.description: An object containing fingerprint signatures. This will be available only if the client is using CloudFlare. The JA3/JA4 fingerprint can be null or empty in some cases. The most common case is for HTTP requests because JA3 and JA4 are calculated in TLS. It can also be empty due to the Worker sending requests within the same zone or to a zone that is not proxied (or a third party).

ja3.description: JA3 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

ja4.description: JA4 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

tenant.description: Details about the Tenant associated with the current transaction.

acr_values.description: Any acr_values provided in the original authentication request.

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID can be provided in the initial authentication request when the application redirects to Universal Login. You can use value to correlate logs and requests from your Action code with the user flow.

locale.description: The locale to be used for this transaction as determined by comparing the browser's requested languages to the tenant's language settings.

login_hint.description: Hint to the Authorization Server about the login identifier the End-User might use to log in (if necessary).

prompt.description: List of instructions indicating whether the user may be prompted for re-authentication and consent.

oneOf.description: Most used, web-based login.
Used on mobile devices and single-page apps.
Allows your application to have immediate access to an ID token while still providing for secure and safe retrieval of access and refresh tokens.
SAML protocol used on SaaS apps.
WS-Federation used on Microsoft products like Office365.

redirect_uri.description: The URL to which Auth0 will redirect the browser after the transaction is completed.

requested_scopes.description: The scopes requested (if any) when starting this authentication flow.

response_mode.description: Informs the Authorization Server of the mechanism to be used for returning parameters from the Authorization Endpoint.

response_type.description: Denotes the kind of credential that Auth0 will return.

state.description: An opaque arbitrary alphanumeric string your app adds to the initial request that Auth0 includes when redirecting back to your application.

ui_locales.description: The ui_locales provided in the original authentication request.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

user.description: An object describing the user on whose behalf the current transaction was initiated.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

custom_hash_required.description: Indicates whether a custom hash computation is required.

custom_password_hash.description: Information about the custom password hash from the bulk import.

hash.description: The password hash to compare against the plaintext password.

encoding.description: The encoding of the hash value.

universal_hash_metadata.description: Optional base64-encoded metadata related to the password hash. You are responsible for encoding and decoding this value.

universal_hash_name.description: A custom identifier for the hash algorithm used. This allows you to implement different hash computations based on this value.

plaintext_password.description: The plaintext password provided by the user at login.

tenant.description: Details about the Tenant associated with the current transaction.

PasswordResetPostChallenge.title: PasswordResetPostChallengeV1Event
PasswordResetPostChallenge.description: Event Object for the Password Reset Post Challenge

authentication.description: Details about authentication obtained during the password reset flow.

methods.description: Contains the authentication methods a user has completed during their session.

name.description:
The name of the first factor that was completed. Values include the following:
- `federated` A social or enterprise connection was used to authenticate the user as the first factor.
- `pwd` A password was used to authenticate a database connection user as the first factor.
- `passkey` A passkey was used to authenticate a database connection user as the first factor.
- `sms` A Passwordless SMS connection was used to authenticate the user as the first factor.
- `email` A Passwordless Email connection was used to authenticate the user as the first factor or verify email for password reset.
- `phone_number` A phone number was used for password reset.
- `mock` Used for internal testing.
- May also be a URL denoting a custom authentication method (as second or later factor).
@summary First Factor

The user completed multi-factor authentication (second or later factors).
@summary Multi-factor Authentication

riskAssessment.description: Supplemental risk assessment. This is available only if the Akamai Integration is enabled and Akamai forwards the headers for the transaction.

supplemental.description: Supplemental signals sent from third party providers to assist in risk assessments.

akamai.description: [Early Access] Supplemental risk assessment. This is available only if Akamai Account Protector is enabled and Akamai forwards the headers for the transaction.

akamaiBot.description: The bot detection results as forwarded by Akamai Bot Manager.

type.description: The type of the Akamai bot manager results.

action.description: The action of the Akamai bot manager results.

botCategory.description: The bot category of the Akamai bot manager results.

botScore.description: The bot score of the Akamai bot manager results.

botScoreResponseSegment.description: The bot score response segment of the Akamai bot manager results.

botnetId.description: The botnet ID of the Akamai bot manager results.

akamaiUserRisk.description: The user risk detection results as forwarded by Akamai Account Protector.

action.description: The action of the Akamai user risk assessment.

allow.description: The allowed status of the Akamai user risk assessment.

emailDomain.description: The email domain of the user.

general.description: The general risk of the Akamai user risk assessment.

requestid.description: The request ID of the user.

risk.description: The risk of the Akamai user risk assessment.

status.description: The status of the Akamai user risk assessment.

trust.description: The trust of the Akamai user risk assessment.

uuid.description: The UUID of the Akamai user risk assessment.

created_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was first created.

email_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their email address.

last_password_reset.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's password was reset/changed. At user creation, this field does not exist. This property is only available for Database connections.

name.description: User's full name.

phone_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their phone number.

updated_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's profile was last updated/modified.

user_id.description: (unique) User's unique identifier.

user_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that does not impact what they can or cannot access, such as work address, home address, or user preferences.

enrolledFactors.description: An array of authentication factors that the user has enrolled. Empty array means the user has no enrolled factors.  If enrolledFactors is undefined, the system was unable fetch the information, the user may or may not have enrolled factors.

items.description: An object describing an enrolled authentication factor type and any factor-specific options.

type.description: The type of authentication factor such as `push-notification`, `phone`, `email`, `otp`, `webauthn-roaming` and `webauthn-platform`.

options.description: Additional options describing this instance of the enrolled factor.

identities.description: Contains info retrieved from the identity provider with which the user originally authenticated. Users may also link their profile to multiple identity providers; those identities will then also appear in this array. The contents of an individual identity provider object varies by provider.

connection.description: Name of the Auth0 connection used to authenticate the user.

isSocial.description: Indicates whether the connection is a social one.

profileData.description: User information associated with the connection. When profiles are linked, it is populated with the associated user info for secondary accounts.

provider.description: Name of the entity that is authenticating the user, such as Facebook, Google, SAML, or your own provider.

user_id.description: User's unique identifier for this connection/provider.

authentication.description: Details about supplemental authentication signals obtained during the password change flow.

riskAssessment.description: Details about risk assessments information for different flows.

supplemental.description: Supplemental signals sent from third party providers to assist in risk assessments.

akamai.description: [Early Access] Supplemental risk assessment. This is available only if Akamai Account Protector is enabled and Akamai forwards the headers for the transaction.

akamaiBot.description: The bot detection results as forwarded by Akamai Bot Manager.

type.description: The type of the Akamai bot manager results.

action.description: The action of the Akamai bot manager results.

botCategory.description: The bot category of the Akamai bot manager results.

botScore.description: The bot score of the Akamai bot manager results.

botScoreResponseSegment.description: The bot score response segment of the Akamai bot manager results.

botnetId.description: The botnet ID of the Akamai bot manager results.

akamaiUserRisk.description: The user risk detection results as forwarded by Akamai Account Protector.

action.description: The action of the Akamai user risk assessment.

allow.description: The allowed status of the Akamai user risk assessment.

emailDomain.description: The email domain of the user.

general.description: The general risk of the Akamai user risk assessment.

requestid.description: The request ID of the user.

risk.description: The risk of the Akamai user risk assessment.

score.description: The score of the Akamai user risk assessment.

status.description: The status of the Akamai user risk assessment.

trust.description: The trust of the Akamai user risk assessment.

uuid.description: The UUID of the Akamai user risk assessment.

connection.description: Details about the Connection that was used for the current transaction.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

tenant.description: Details about the Tenant associated with the current transaction.

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID can be provided in the initial authentication request when the application redirects to Universal Login. You can use value to correlate logs and requests from your Action code with the user flow.

user.description: An object describing the user on whose behalf the current transaction was initiated.

email_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their email address.

last_password_reset.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's password was reset/changed. At user creation, this field does not exist. This property is only available for Database connections.

phone_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their phone number.

user_id.description: (unique) User's unique identifier.

authentication.description: Details about authentication signals obtained during the login flow.

methods.description: Contains the authentication methods a user has completed during their session.

name.description:
The name of the first factor that was completed. Values include the following:
- `federated` A social or enterprise connection was used to authenticate the user as the first factor.
- `pwd` A password was used to authenticate a database connection user as the first factor.
- `passkey` A passkey was used to authenticate a database connection user as the first factor.
- `sms` A Passwordless SMS connection was used to authenticate the user as the first factor.
- `email` A Passwordless Email connection was used to authenticate the user as the first factor or verify email for password reset.
- `phone_number` A phone number was used for password reset.
- `mock` Used for internal testing.
- May also be a URL denoting a custom authentication method (as second or later factor).
@summary First Factor

The user completed multi-factor authentication (second or later factors).
@summary Multi-factor Authentication

riskAssessment.description: Details about risk assessments obtained during the login or password reset flow.

ImpossibleTravel.description: Determines if the user is logging in from a location signaling impossible travel.

NewDevice.description: Determines if the user is logging in from a known device.

UntrustedIP.description: Shows if the IP was found in Auth0's repository of low reputation IPs.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

akamaiBot.description: The bot detection results as forwarded by Akamai Bot Manager.

type.description: The type of the Akamai bot manager results.

action.description: The action of the Akamai bot manager results.

botCategory.description: The bot category of the Akamai bot manager results.

botScore.description: The bot score of the Akamai bot manager results.

botScoreResponseSegment.description: The bot score response segment of the Akamai bot manager results.

botnetId.description: The botnet ID of the Akamai bot manager results.

akamaiUserRisk.description: The user risk detection results as forwarded by Akamai Account Protector.

action.description: The action of the Akamai user risk assessment.

allow.description: The allowed status of the Akamai user risk assessment.

emailDomain.description: The email domain of the user.

general.description: The general risk of the Akamai user risk assessment.

requestid.description: The request ID of the user.

risk.description: The risk of the Akamai user risk assessment.

score.description: The score of the Akamai user risk assessment.

status.description: The status of the Akamai user risk assessment.

trust.description: The trust of the Akamai user risk assessment.

uuid.description: The UUID of the Akamai user risk assessment.

authorization.description: An object containing information describing the authorization granted to the user who is logging in.

roles.description: An array containing the names of a user's assigned roles.

client.description: Information about the Client with which this login transaction was initiated.

client_id.description: The client id of the application to which the user is logging in.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

name.description: The name of the application (as defined in the Dashboard).

refresh_token.description: [Early Access] An object for holding refresh token configuration properties.

policies.description: [Early Access] A collection of policies governing multi-resource refresh token exchange (MRRT), defining how refresh tokens can be used across different resource servers

audience.description: [Early Access] The specific resource server (audience) to which this MRRT policy applies.

scope.description: The scopes of access that are authorized for the resource server (audience).

items.description: [Early Access] A single scope of access (e.g., read, write) granted to the resource server.

connection.description: Details about the Connection that was used to authenticate the user.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

organization.description: Details about the Organization associated with the current transaction.

display_name.description: The friendly name of the Organization.

name.description: The name of the Organization.

prompt.description: Collected data from rendered custom prompts.

id.description: [Enterprise Customers] The ID of the refresh token.

client_id.description: [Enterprise Customers] The ID of the client associated with the refresh token.

created_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] Timestamp of when the refresh token was created.

initial_asn.description: [Enterprise Customers] First autonomous system number associated with this refresh token.

initial_ip.description: [Enterprise Customers] First IP address associated with this refresh token.

initial_user_agent.description: [Enterprise Customers] First user agent of the device associated with this refresh token.

last_asn.description: [Enterprise Customers] Last autonomous system number from which this refresh token was last exchanged.

last_ip.description: [Enterprise Customers] Last IP address from which this refresh token was last exchanged.

last_user_agent.description: [Enterprise Customers] Last user agent of the device from which this refresh token was last exchanged.

expires_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] Timestamp of when the refresh token will absolutely expire.

idle_expires_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] Timestamp of when the refresh token will idle expire.

last_exchanged_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] Timestamp of when the refresh token was last successfully exchanged.

audience.description: [Enterprise Customers] The audience of the refresh token.

scopes.description: [Enterprise Customers] Scopes of the refresh token.

rotating.description: [Enterprise Customers] If the refresh token is a rotating refresh token.

session_id.description: [Enterprise Customers] The ID of the session bound to the refresh token.

session_transfer.description: [Enterprise Customers] This object is defined when the session is created from a session transfer token (Native to Web SSO), undefined otherwise.

parent_refresh_token.description: [Enterprise Customers] This object is defined when the refresh token is created from a session initiated as a result of session transfer (Native to Web SSO), undefined otherwise.

id.description: [Enterprise Customers] The ID of the parent refresh token from which this session/refresh token was created as a result of a session transfer (Native to Web SSO).

user_id.description: [Enterprise Customers] The ID of the user bound to the refresh token.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

asn.description: The ASN (autonomous system number) of the user-agent making the request.

body.description: The body of the POST request. This data will only be available during refresh token and Client Credential Exchange flows and Post Login Action.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

query.description: The query string parameters sent to the authorization request.

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

resource_server.description: Details about the resource server to which the access is being requested.

identifier.description: The identifier of the resource server. For example: `https://your-api.example.com`.

security_context.description: An object containing fingerprint signatures. This will be available only if the client is using cloudflare. The JA3/JA4 fingerprint can be null or empty in some cases. The most common case is for HTTP requests because JA3 and JA4 are calculated in TLS. It can also be empty due to the Worker sending requests within the same zone or to a zone that is not proxied (or a third party).

ja3.description: JA3 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

ja4.description: JA4 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

id.description: The ID of the current session.

authenticated_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] The date and time when the session was last authenticated.

clients.description: [Enterprise Customers] List of client details for the session.

client_id.description: [Enterprise Customers] ID of client for the session.

cookie.description: [Enterprise Customers] Cookie configuration for the session, which determines how the session cookie is handled by the User Agent.

mode.description: [Enterprise Customers] The persistence mode of the session cookie. When set to 'non-persistent' (ephemeral), the cookie will be deleted when the browser is closed. When set to 'persistent', the cookie will be stored until it expires or is deleted by the user.

created_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] The date and time when the session was created.

device.description: [Enterprise Customers] Metadata related to the device used in the session.

initial_asn.description: [Enterprise Customers] First autonomous system number associated with this session.

initial_ip.description: [Enterprise Customers] First IP address associated with this session.

initial_user_agent.description: [Enterprise Customers] First user agent of the device associated with this session.

last_asn.description: [Enterprise Customers] Last autonomous system number from which this user logged in.

last_ip.description: [Enterprise Customers] Last IP address from which this user logged in.

last_user_agent.description: [Enterprise Customers] Last user agent of the device from which this user logged in.

expires_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] The date and time when the session will expire.

idle_expires_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] The date and time when the session will expire if idle.

last_interacted_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] The date and time when the session was last successfully interacted with.

session_transfer.description: [Enterprise Customers] [Early Access] This object is defined when the session is created from a session transfer token (Native to Web SSO), undefined otherwise.

parent_refresh_token.description: [Enterprise Customers] This object is defined when the refresh token is created from a session initiated as a result of session transfer (Native to Web SSO), undefined otherwise.

id.description: [Enterprise Customers] The ID of the parent refresh token from which this session/refresh token was created as a result of a session transfer (Native to Web SSO).

metadata.description: [Enterprise Customers] The metadata of the parent refresh token from which this session/refresh token was created as a result of a session transfer (Native to Web SSO).

updated_at.description: [Enterprise Customers] The date and time when the session was last updated.

user_id.description: [Enterprise Customers] ID of the user which can be used when interacting with other APIs.

session_transfer_token.description: [Early Access] Details of the current session transfer token being used to establish Single Sign-On (SSO) from a native application to a web application.

client_id.description: [Early Access] The client identifier of the application that issued the token.

request.description: [Early Access] Details about the request that issued the token.

asn.description: [Early Access] The Autonomous System Number (ASN) associated with the request that issued the token.

ip.description: [Early Access] The IP address associated with the request that issued the token.

user_agent.description: [Early Access] The User-Agent string of the device that issued the token.

scope.description: [Early Access] The scopes requested when the token was issued.

stats.description: Login statistics for the current user.

logins_count.description: The number of times this user has logged in.

tenant.description: Details about the Tenant associated with the current transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the transaction. Populated for all browser-based login flows.

acr_values.description: Any acr_values provided in the original authentication request.

linking_id.description: Dynamic Linking ID that allows developers to reference this transaction.

locale.description: The locale to be used for this transaction as determined by comparing the browser's requested languages to the tenant's language settings.

login_hint.description: Hint to the Authorization Server about the login identifier the End-User might use to log in (if necessary).

prompt.description: List of instructions indicating whether the user may be prompted for re-authentication and consent.

oneOf.description: Most used, web-based login.
Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) using mobile push authorization.
Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) using web-based authorization.
Used on mobile devices and single-page apps.
Transaction using the Device Authorization Flow.
User/password login typically used on database connections.
Login using a bearer JWT signed with user's private key.
Login using the password exchange.
Login using the webauthn exchange.
@deprecated Getting a token with a Social Provider's access token (more details [here](https://auth0.com/docs/api/authentication/login-legacy/social-with-providers-access-token)).
Refreshing a token using the refresh token exchange.
Exchanging an assertion for an Auth0-issued token.
Allows your application to have immediate access to an ID token while still providing for secure and safe retrieval of access and refresh tokens.
SAML protocol used on SaaS apps.
WS-Federation used on Microsoft products like Office365.
WS-trust User/password login used on CRM and Office365.

redirect_uri.description: The URL to which Auth0 will redirect the browser after the transaction is completed.

requested_authorization_details.description: The details of a rich authorization request per Section 2 of the Rich Authorization Requests spec at https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-rar#section-2.

type.description: The type of authorization details as a string. The value of the type field determines the allowable contents of the object which contains it.

requested_scopes.description: The scopes requested (if any) when starting this authentication flow.

response_mode.description: Informs the Authorization Server of the mechanism to be used for returning parameters from the Authorization Endpoint.

response_type.description: Denotes the kind of credential that Auth0 will return.

state.description: An opaque arbitrary alphanumeric string your app adds to the initial request that Auth0 includes when redirecting back to your application.

ui_locales.description: The ui_locales provided in the original authentication request.

actor.description: The actor in a token exchange request.
actor.$comment: Value-type constraints for custom properties are enforced programmatically in the CTE trigger API and server-side, not in this schema, to avoid TS codegen conflicts with the nested act property.

act.description: The next actor in the delegation chain, representing that this actor is making the request on behalf of another principal.
The next actor in the delegation chain, representing that this actor is making the request on behalf of another principal.
The next actor in the delegation chain, representing that this actor is making the request on behalf of another principal.
The next actor in the delegation chain, representing that this actor is making the request on behalf of another principal.

sub.description: The subject identifier of the actor. A unique identifier for the entity acting in this role.
The subject identifier of the actor. A unique identifier for the entity acting in this role.
The subject identifier of the actor. A unique identifier for the entity acting in this role.
The subject identifier of the actor. A unique identifier for the entity acting in this role.
The subject identifier of the actor. A unique identifier for the entity acting in this role.

metadata.description: An object containing shared data across custom Actions for the duration of a transaction.

subject_token_type.description: The type of the subject token in a token exchange request.

connection.description: Details about the Connection that was used to register the user.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

body.description: The body of the POST request. This data will only be available during refresh token, Client Credential Exchange flows and PreUserRegistration Action.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

security_context.description: An object containing fingerprint signatures. This will be available only if the client is using cloudflare. The JA3/JA4 fingerprint can be null or empty in some cases. The most common case is for HTTP requests because JA3 and JA4 are calculated in TLS. It can also be empty due to the Worker sending requests within the same zone or to a zone that is not proxied (or a third party).

ja3.description: JA3 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

ja4.description: JA4 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

tenant.description: Details about the Tenant associated with the current transaction.

transaction_metadata.description: An object containing shared data across custom Actions for the duration of a transaction.

acr_values.description: Any acr_values provided in the original authentication request.

locale.description: The locale to be used for this transaction as determined by comparing the browser's requested languages to the tenant's language settings.

login_hint.description: Hint to the Authorization Server about the login identifier the End-User might use to log in (if necessary).

prompt.description: List of instructions indicating whether the user may be prompted for re-authentication and consent.

oneOf.description: Most used, web-based login.
Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) using mobile push authorization.
Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) using web-based authorization.
Used on mobile devices and single-page apps.
Transaction using the Device Authorization Flow.
User/password login typically used on database connections.
Login using a bearer JWT signed with user's private key.
Login using the password exchange.
Login using the webauthn exchange.
@deprecated Getting a token with a Social Provider's access token (more details [here](https://auth0.com/docs/api/authentication/login-legacy/social-with-providers-access-token)).
Refreshing a token using the refresh token exchange.
Exchanging an assertion for an Auth0-issued token.
Allows your application to have immediate access to an ID token while still providing for secure and safe retrieval of access and refresh tokens.
SAML protocol used on SaaS apps.
WS-Federation used on Microsoft products like Office365.
WS-trust User/password login used on CRM and Office365.

redirect_uri.description: The URL to which Auth0 will redirect the browser after the transaction is completed.

requested_scopes.description: The scopes requested (if any) when starting this authentication flow.

response_mode.description: Informs the Authorization Server of the mechanism to be used for returning parameters from the Authorization Endpoint.

response_type.description: Denotes the kind of credential that Auth0 will return.

state.description: An opaque arbitrary alphanumeric string your app adds to the initial request that Auth0 includes when redirecting back to your application.

ui_locales.description: The ui_locales provided in the original authentication request.

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID can be provided in the initial authentication request when the application redirects to Universal Login. You can use value to correlate logs and requests from your Action code with the user flow.

user.description: An object describing the user who is attempting to register.

app_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that influences the user's access, such as support plan, security roles, or access control groups.

name.description: User's full name.

user_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that does not impact what they can or cannot access, such as work address, home address, or user preferences.

client.description: Information about the Client with which this transaction was initiated.

client_id.description: The client id of the application the user is logging in to.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

name.description: The name of the application (as defined in the Dashboard).

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

message_options.description: Details about the message that is sent to the user.

action.description: The flow that triggered this action.

code.description: One-time password that the user needs to use to enter in the form.

message_type.description: How the message will be delivered, either by 'sms' or 'voice'.

recipient.description: Phone number where the message will be sent.

text.description: Content of the message to be sent.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

security_context.description: An object containing fingerprint signatures. This will be available only if the client is using cloudflare. The JA3/JA4 fingerprint can be null or empty in some cases. The most common case is for HTTP requests because JA3 and JA4 are calculated in TLS. It can also be empty due to the Worker sending requests within the same zone or to a zone that is not proxied (or a third party).

ja3.description: JA3 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

ja4.description: JA4 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

tenant.description: Details about the Tenant associated with the current transaction.

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID can be provided in the initial authentication request when the application redirects to Universal Login. You can use value to correlate logs and requests from your Action code with the user flow.

user.description: An object describing the user on whose behalf the current transaction was initiated.

app_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that influences the user's access, such as support plan, security roles, or access control groups.

created_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was first created.

email_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their email address.

last_password_reset.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's password was reset/changed. At user creation, this field does not exist. This property is only available for Database connections.

name.description: User's full name.

phone_verified.description: Indicates whether the user has verified their phone number.

updated_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's profile was last updated/modified.

user_id.description: (unique) User's unique identifier.

user_metadata.description: Custom fields that store info about a user that does not impact what they can or cannot access, such as work address, home address, or user preferences.

identities.description: Contains info retrieved from the identity provider with which the user originally authenticates. Users may also link their profile to multiple identity providers; those identities will then also appear in this array. The contents of an individual identity provider object varies by provider.

connection.description: Name of the Auth0 connection used to authenticate the user.

isSocial.description: Indicates whether the connection is a social one.

profileData.description: User information associated with the connection. When profiles are linked, it is populated with the associated user info for secondary accounts.

provider.description: Name of the entity that is authenticating the user, such as Facebook, Google, SAML, or your own provider.

user_id.description: User's unique identifier for this connection/provider.

client.description: Information about the Client with which this signup transaction was initiated.

client_id.description: The client id of the application the user is logging in to.

metadata.description: An object for holding other application properties.

name.description: The name of the application (as defined in the Dashboard).

custom_domain.description: Details about the custom domain associated with the current transaction.

domain_metadata.description: Custom domain metadata as key-value pairs.

initial_connection.description: Details about the Connection that was specified to authenticate the user. This represents the initial value as resolved by Auth0 prior to executing this trigger. Calling setConnectionByName will not change this value because it is immutable.

name.description: The name of the connection used to authenticate the user (such as `twitter` or `some-g-suite-domain`).

strategy.description: The type of connection. For social connections, `event.connection.strategy === event.connection.name`. For enterprise connections, the strategy is `waad` (Windows Azure AD), `ad` (Active Directory/LDAP), `auth0` (database connections), and so on.

initial_organization.description: Details about the Organization associated with the current transaction. This represents the initial value as resolved by Auth0 prior to executing this trigger. Calling setOrganization will not change this value because it is immutable.

display_name.description: The friendly name of the Organization.

name.description: The name of the Organization.

request.description: Details about the request that initiated the transaction.

asn.description: The ASN (autonomous system number) of the user-agent making the request.

hostname.description: The hostname that is being used for the authentication flow.

ip.description: The originating IP address of the request.

language.description: The language requested by the browser.

method.description: The HTTP method used for the request

query.description: The query string parameters sent to the authorization request.

user_agent.description: The value of the `User-Agent` header received when initiating the transaction.

resource_server.description: Details about the resource server to which the access is being requested.

identifier.description: The identifier of the resource server. For example: `https://your-api.example.com`.

security_context.description: An object containing fingerprint signatures. This will be available only if the client is using CloudFlare. The JA3/JA4 fingerprint can be null or empty in some cases. The most common case is for HTTP requests because JA3 and JA4 are calculated in TLS. It can also be empty due to the Worker sending requests within the same zone or to a zone that is not proxied (or a third party).

ja3.description: JA3 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

ja4.description: JA4 fingerprint signature. This will be available only if the client is using a TLS connection.

tenant.description: Details about the Tenant associated with the current transaction.

acr_values.description: Any acr_values provided in the original authentication request.

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID can be provided in the initial authentication request when the application redirects to Universal Login. You can use value to correlate logs and requests from your Action code with the user flow.

locale.description: The locale to be used for this transaction as determined by comparing the browser's requested languages to the tenant's language settings.

login_hint.description: Hint to the Authorization Server about the login identifier the End-User might use to log in (if necessary).

prompt.description: List of instructions indicating whether the user may be prompted for re-authentication and consent.

Used on mobile devices and single-page apps.
Allows your application to have immediate access to an ID token while still providing for secure and safe retrieval of access and refresh tokens.
SAML protocol used on SaaS apps.
WS-Federation used on Microsoft products like Office365.

requested_scopes.description: The scopes requested (if any) when starting this authentication flow.

response_mode.description: Informs the Authorization Server of the mechanism to be used for returning parameters from the Authorization Endpoint.

response_type.description: Denotes the kind of credential that Auth0 will return.
